QUYANNA CARE CENTER

CCN: 025026 · NOME, AK 99762 · Nome County

Address
1100 GREG KRUSCHEK AVENUE, NOME, AK 99762
Phone
9074433311
Certified beds
18
Avg daily residents
18 (98% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit corporation
Provider type

Medicare/Medicaid since

Setting
Rural
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 8.61 3.06 10.86 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 2.76 0.54 3.47 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.83 0.68 1.04 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 5.03 1.85 6.34 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 3.58 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.01 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
6.94
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
2.19
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
8.76
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.08
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.79
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-10-04 7 7 0 68 1 68
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-06-09 13 13 0 132 1 132
Total weighted health score
84.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (26)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

26 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0678 Provide basic life support, including CPR, prior to the arrival of emergency medical personnel , subject to physician orders and the resident’s advance directives. D 2024-10-04 2024-11-18
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. E 2024-10-04 2024-11-18
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. F 2024-10-04 2024-11-18
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. F 2024-10-04 2024-11-18
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-10-04 2024-11-18
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-10-04 2024-11-18
0915 Ensure each resident room has a window to the outside that meets requirements D 2024-10-04 2024-11-18
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2023-06-09 2023-07-24
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. E 2023-06-09 2023-07-24
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2023-06-09 2023-07-24
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2023-06-09 2023-07-24
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2023-06-09 2023-07-24
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2023-06-09 2023-07-24
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2023-06-09 2023-07-24
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. E 2023-06-09 2023-07-24
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. F 2023-06-09 2023-07-24
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2023-06-09 2023-07-24
0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. F 2023-06-09 2023-07-24
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2023-06-09 2023-07-24
0949 Provide behavior health training consistent with the requirements and as determined by a facility assessment. F 2023-06-09 2023-07-24
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. D 2022-08-15 2022-09-29
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2022-08-15 2022-09-29
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2022-08-15 2022-09-29
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2022-08-15 2022-09-29
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2022-08-15 2022-09-29
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2022-08-15 2022-09-29
